old_claim.note: Software Engineering provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Software engineering is a generic implementation discipline, not a distinct technical prerequisite of the X.509 certificate and trust model.

source_shape.source_locator: Recommendation record for X.509 (11/88), The Directory - Authentication framework, approved 25 November 1988.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The originating standards record identifies X.509 as an authentication framework within the international Directory standards, not as a software-engineering method.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Implementations naturally use software practices, but that broad discipline does not distinguish X.509 infrastructure from other implemented systems and adds no certificate-specific causal mechanism.

ontology_before: A generic discipline used to implement many technologies was modeled as a direct enabling cause of PKI.
ontology_after: The direct PKI boundary contains its public-key and signature mechanisms; software engineering remains general implementation background.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-public-key-infrastructure-x509-boundary: software_engineering is absent.
would_reject_if: An originating X.509 specification identifies a particular software-engineering method as indispensable to the certificate trust architecture.
short_reason: The X.509 origin record defines an authentication framework, while generic software engineering is implementation background rather than a PKI prerequisite.
